summary: Policy-backed control for lock-screen toast notifications. The local Microsoft WPN.admx file explicitly documents the same user policy key, value name, and enabled/disabled values that the app uses.

setting.name: Lock Screen Toast Notifications
setting.casual_explanation: Windows can show some app notifications directly on the lock screen. This policy decides whether apps can raise those lock-screen toast alerts for the current user.
setting.why_people_change_it: People disable lock-screen notifications to keep sensitive information off the lock screen and make the sign-in surface quieter.
setting.tradeoffs_overview: Turning this off improves lock-screen privacy, but apps can no longer show toast notifications there.
setting.professional_notes: Microsoft documents NoLockScreenToastNotification in ADMX_WPN. The app uses the user-side policy path HKCU\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\PushNotifications\NoToastApplicationNotificationOnLockScreen = 1, which matches the documented disable behavior.

allowed_values.label: Not configured
allowed_values.meaning: Applications can still raise toast notifications on the lock screen under normal Windows behavior.

allowed_values.label: Lock-screen toasts allowed
allowed_values.meaning: The policy is disabled and apps can still raise toast notifications on the lock screen.

allowed_values.label: Lock-screen toasts turned off
allowed_values.meaning: Applications cannot raise toast notifications on the lock screen.

app_current_implementation.notes: The current app writes NoToastApplicationNotificationOnLockScreen = 1 for the current user, which matches the documented disable behavior.

windows_defaults.label: Windows user-policy default
windows_defaults.applies_to: Supported Windows editions with WPN lock-screen notification policy support

states.rationale: The user policy is not configured by default.

windows_defaults.notes: Microsoft documents the functional default as lock-screen toast notifications allowed when the policy is not configured.

recommended_profiles.label: Windows default
intended_for: People who want normal lock-screen notification behavior
recommended_profiles.good_when: You want apps to keep surfacing supported alerts on the lock screen.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You want the lock screen to reveal less information.
tradeoffs: No policy-based privacy restriction is enforced.

recommended_profiles.label: Private lock screen
avoid_for: Users who rely on lock-screen alerts

states.rationale: Block app toast notifications on the lock screen.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want less information exposed before sign-in.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You rely on lock-screen toasts for awareness.
tradeoffs: The lock screen becomes quieter and more private.
Apps lose their ability to surface toast alerts there.

evidence.title: Microsoft Policy CSP: ADMX_WPN / NoLockScreenToastNotification
evidence.summary: Official Microsoft documentation for the policy that turns off app toast notifications on the lock screen.

evidence.title: Local Microsoft WPN.admx NoLockScreenToastNotification mapping
evidence.summary: The local ADMX file maps NoLockScreenToastNotification to the PushNotifications policy key with enabledValue 1 and disabledValue 0.

evidence.title: Local Microsoft WPN.adml NoLockScreenToastNotification help text
evidence.summary: The local ADML help text says enabling the policy turns off toast notifications on the lock screen.

evidence.title: Current app implementation
evidence.summary: The provider writes NoToastApplicationNotificationOnLockScreen = 1 for the current tweak.

decision.why: The local Microsoft WPN.admx file explicitly defines NoLockScreenToastNotification at SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\PushNotifications with valueName NoToastApplicationNotificationOnLockScreen and enabledValue 1 / disabledValue 0, which matches the app's user policy surface.

validation_proof.notes: The local official Microsoft ADMX file names the same user policy key and value name that the app writes and confirms the 1/0 mapping.
